title: Offline survey queue silently drops entries when device storage is full
description: When local device storage falls below 50MB, the offline queue discards new survey entries without notifying the volunteer. Field testers at Kaneohe Bay lost 3 transect entries before the bug was caught. Need storage pre-check and user-facing warning before submission.

title: Background sync does not retry after airplane mode toggle
description: When a volunteer exits airplane mode, the background sync task does not fire automatically. They must manually open the app and tap Sync. This breaks the expected behavior of passive syncing when connectivity resumes at the boat dock.

title: Survey form GPS coordinates capture shows stale location after device sleep
description: GPS coordinate field populates from cached location if the device has been idle for more than 5 minutes. Volunteers filling out forms after wading to a new transect marker get wrong coordinates. Needs fresh location request on field focus.

title: Photo upload fails silently for images over 8MB on iOS
description: High-res photos from iPhone 15 Pro (often 12–18MB) fail during upload with no error shown to the user. The upload spinner just stops. Android users on the same network succeed. Need client-side resize before upload or a clear error with retry prompt.

title: Survey form required field validation allows submission with blank coral health score
description: Coral health score (1–5 scale) is marked required in the spec but the submit button is not blocked when it is blank. Submitted records arrive in the database with null health scores, breaking the coordinator export query.

title: Species ID search returns no results for Hawaiian common names
description: Searching for species by Hawaiian common name (e.g., 'humuhumunukunukuapua'a', 'manini') returns zero results even when the species exists in the database under its scientific name. Need fuzzy alias lookup for Hawaiian common names in the species index.

title: Map of monitoring sites does not cluster pins below zoom level 12
description: At zoom level 11 and below, all monitoring site pins render individually and the map becomes unresponsive on lower-end Android devices. Implement marker clustering so dense survey areas like Hanauma Bay and Kaneohe Bay render without lag.

title: Add Hawaiian language support for survey form field labels
description: Many volunteer surveyors in the Ka'a'awa and Waikane communities prefer Hawaiian-language labels. Localization strings for Hawaiian (haw) need to be added for all survey form fields and error messages. Nalani has the translation glossary ready.

title: Battery drains 40% per hour during active field survey session
description: Profiling shows the GPS polling interval is set to 2 seconds regardless of whether the survey form is actively being edited. Reducing poll rate to 10s when form is idle and 2s only on coordinate field focus should cut battery draw significantly.

title: Coordinator dashboard: survey count cards show stale data until hard refresh
description: The summary cards on the coordinator dashboard (total surveys submitted, active sites, species recorded this week) do not auto-update when new sync events arrive. Data is stale until the coordinator manually refreshes the page.

title: Export survey data to CSV from coordinator dashboard
description: Coordinators need a one-click CSV export of all survey records for a date range and site filter. Output columns: survey_id, site_name, volunteer_name, date, gps_lat, gps_lon, coral_health_score, bleaching_pct, species_count, notes. Required for quarterly reporting to DLNR.

title: Push notification opt-in prompt never appears for new volunteer installs
description: New installs skip the push notification permission prompt entirely. The OS-level permission is never requested. Volunteers miss reminders for scheduled survey windows at their assigned sites. Trace shows the prompt call is behind a feature flag that defaults off in production.

title: Survey form tablet layout: input fields too narrow on 10-inch landscape
description: On 10-inch tablets in landscape orientation the survey form renders in a single column matching the phone layout. Fields should expand to use two columns on tablets wider than 900px. Volunteers using iPads in the field have reported difficulty tapping the narrow inputs.

title: Species photo gallery: thumbnail grid breaks when more than 12 photos attached
description: Survey records with more than 12 attached photos cause the thumbnail grid to overflow its container and push the Submit button off screen. Capping display at 12 with a '+N more' affordance is an acceptable interim fix.

title: Increase base font size for field legibility in bright sunlight
description: Volunteers report that the default 13px body font is unreadable in direct Hawaiian sunlight on reflective screens. Bumping base body to 16px and input labels to 15px should bring it in line with WCAG AA for outdoor readability. Check contrast ratios after change.

title: Species ID: add confidence score display for AI-assisted identification
description: The AI species ID model returns a confidence percentage but the app only shows the top match name. Displaying the confidence score (e.g. '92% — Porites lobata') helps volunteers decide whether to override the suggestion or accept it. Design mockup in Figma board.

title: Sync conflict resolution: last-write-wins overwrites earlier valid entry
description: When two volunteers submit surveys for the same site within the same sync window, the conflict resolver always takes the later timestamp, discarding the earlier record entirely. Both records are valid and should be stored as separate entries keyed by volunteer ID.

title: Map view: offline tile cache does not pre-fetch adjacent zoom levels
description: When a volunteer downloads an offline map area, only the current zoom level tiles are cached. Pinching to zoom in on a survey site while offline shows blank tiles. Pre-fetching ±1 zoom levels for the downloaded bounding box would resolve most reported cases.

title: Drop duplicate species entry form — consolidate into unified survey flow
description: There are currently two separate entry points for recording species: one in the main survey form and a standalone Species Log screen. Volunteers are confused about which to use. Design decision: remove the standalone screen and surface species entry as a section within the main survey form.

title: Coral bleaching percentage input: allow decimal entry (e.g. 12.5%)
description: The bleaching percentage field currently only accepts integers. Scientific surveys often record fractional bleaching estimates (e.g. 12.5%, 33.3%). Field should accept one decimal place. Update validation and backend schema accordingly.

title: Volunteer onboarding: site assignment screen has no empty state when no sites are assigned
description: New volunteers who have not yet been assigned a monitoring site by their coordinator see a blank white screen with no explanation. Add an empty state with a friendly message and a contact coordinator CTA.

title: Photo upload: show upload progress bar during sync
description: When photos are uploading during sync, there is no progress indicator. On slow LTE connections near Waimanalo the upload can take 30–60 seconds and volunteers assume the app has frozen. A per-photo progress bar or overall sync percentage would reduce confusion.

title: Map view: tapping a site pin should open a summary card not navigate away
description: Currently tapping a site pin on the map navigates to the full site detail page. Volunteers lose their map scroll position. Desired behavior: show a bottom-sheet summary card (site name, last survey date, volunteer count) with an optional 'Open Full Details' link.

title: Add water temperature field to survey form
description: Reef health scientists need water temperature (°C) recorded at each survey. Field should appear after depth in the abiotic conditions section. Source: temperature data from dive computers or handheld sensors. Jenna confirmed this is required for the DLNR data sharing agreement.
